title: StarCar dispatch-lifecycle artifact
description: Spec S3 preamble: the schema owns six contracts - field names, types, ordering, identity, the index format, and the path-normalisation substitution rule. This file governs fields and types (plus the conditional-requirement shape); ordering, the index format, and the normalisation rule are declared in schema/index-format.md, which this file does not duplicate (Law 6).

kind.description: Recognition vocabulary lives in schema/vocab/kinds.json as DATA, never as a schema enum here (spec S3.2: an unrecognised kind must be a discovery, not a validation failure).

subject.description: THE identity key (ruling R2). For the three dispatch kinds (dispatched, returned, presumed-lost) its value IS the dispatch id. There is no separate dispatch_id field.

at.description: ISO-8601 UTC. Latest-at wins on supersession (spec S3.1).

outcome.description: Recognition vocabulary lives in schema/vocab/outcomes.json as DATA, never as a schema enum here, for the same reason as kind.

abstract.description: The third envelope payload field (spec S2.3), alongside outcome and findings - the human-readable summary a board renders.

envelope.description: Records the envelope fault class. Only meaningful on kind=returned. Absent and malformed are different faults (spec S2.3); both land with the body intact. This is a closed, schema-owned fault-class enum, not a recognition vocabulary, so it is exempt from the vocabularies-as-data rule.

budget.description: Seconds. Optional - the shop default applies at FOLD time, never infinite. The default is the detector's to apply, never the schema's.

producer.description: Optional. Not spec-mandated (S3.4 governs cost/context optionality and names no such field). Basis: Law 7 metadata naming the emitting adapter, optional exactly as cost is.

model.description: Optional, dispatched-only. Issue #26: an observed producer field with clear provenance, not spec-mandated - written by scripts/Produce-Artifact.ps1:285 only when kind=dispatched, sourced from the Task tool_response's resolvedModel (scripts/Produce-Artifact.ps1:204). Same open-posture-extra class as producer/cost. Not part of the canonical field order in schema/index-format.md (a disclosed producer deviation, not an omission).

body_file.description: Optional, returned-only. Issue #26: an observed producer field with clear provenance, not spec-mandated - written by scripts/Migrate-Verdicts.ps1:152 on migrated verdict records, the store-root-relative path to the verdict's landed markdown body. That script's own header (scripts/Migrate-Verdicts.ps1:22) already calls it 'an open-posture extra'. Same open-posture-extra class as producer/cost. Not part of the canonical field order in schema/index-format.md (a disclosed producer deviation, not an omission).

subject_basis.description: Optional. Issue #47 (family-agnostic harness design D2/D4): DISCLOSES which per-family adapter rule produced the subject. 'runtime-id' = mode (b), the runtime's own stable pairing id (Claude's launch/stop UUID); 'minted-id' = mode (a), the shop-minted dispatch id carried in the launch label (Copilot tool_input.name) or the return envelope's task-id. Written by scripts/Produce-Artifact.ps1. Same open-posture-extra class as model/producer; not part of index-format.md's canonical field order (a disclosed producer deviation).

task_id.description: Optional, returned-only. Issue #47 (design section 5.7): the shop-minted dispatch id echoed back by the report's starcar-artifact envelope (`task-id:` line). Lets the minted id join a mode-(b) returned record whose subject is a runtime UUID. Absent when the car emitted no task-id echo. Written by scripts/Produce-Artifact.ps1; same open-posture-extra class as model/producer.

provenance.description: Optional. Issue #47 (D2/D4): runtime-internal ids kept as ENRICHMENT, never identity (identity is the subject). E.g. a Copilot compat stop payload's agent_name, retained beside the minted-id subject. Open-posture extra; not part of index-format.md's canonical field order.

normalisation.description: Declared in each landed artifact. Empty array means nothing was substituted.
